confirmed.note: operator=(RefCountGuard&&) duplicates the entire ~RefCountGuard() cleanup body (reentrant: restore active_ptr + clear outer_stack + fetch_sub count; else: fetch_sub + null active_ptr + clear slot_owner) verbatim; classic dtor/move-assign cleanup copy not factored into a private release() helper

waitForRefCountToClear and waitForAllRefCountsToClear share the same two-phase scaffolding (SPIN_ITERATIONS=100 spinPause loop, then MAX_WAIT_ITERATIONS=5000 nanosleep{0,100000} loop, same all_clear/any break structure); only the inner predicate (slotReferences(target) vs count>0) and the timeout log differ

false_positives.what_happened: Refactor MOVE, not duplication. 'Refactor stackwalker (#451)' physically relocates ~987 lines (getJavaTraceAsync, isAddressInCode, WalkStack helpers) out of profiler.cpp/stackWalker.cpp into the new hotspot/hotspotSupport.cpp, renaming Profiler:: -> HotspotSupport:: and _libs->findLibraryByAddress -> profiler->findLibraryByAddress. The source ranges are DELETED in the same commit (profiler.cpp -401), so there is no surviving second copy. The cross-file detector matched the moved text against the pre-move blob and reported it as cross-file copy-paste. This is exactly the entry the checker hint (CORPUS_CHECK_DETAIL) attributed to dependabot SHA e39e3ad as 'hotspotSupport.cpp <- profiler.cpp'.
false_positives.fix_proposal: In --diff mode, when an ADDED block in file B matches a block that is REMOVED (net-deleted) from file A in the SAME commit, classify as code-move and suppress the copy-paste finding (it is a refactor, not duplication). Concretely: before emitting a cross-file pair, diff both endpoints against the commit's own removed-line set; if the BASE side overlaps deletions >= ~80% of its lines, downgrade to 'moved' and do not count as copypaste.

false_positives.what_happened: Vendored library re-sync, not authored copy-paste. Commit message: 'Update the vendored version of the OTel process context support with the latest code from open-telemetry/sig-profiling#79'. The C-style API (otel_process_ctx_publish/encode_payload/validate_and_calculate_payload_size, write_msgpack_string, packed structs) is copied wholesale from an external reference repo; repeated msgpack encode/size blocks are upstream idioms, and the file is replaced wholesale on each sync, so internal near-duplicate blocks are upstream-authored, not local drift.
false_positives.fix_proposal: Add a vendored-source heuristic to #056: treat a file as vendored (exclude from copypaste) when (a) its public symbols use a single foreign snake_case prefix unlike the repo's dominant CamelCase style AND (b) a commit touching it carries 'vendor'/'sync'/'update ... version of'/'upstream' in the subject. Cheaper alternative: an opt-in .archcheckignore glob for known-vendored paths (otel_process_ctx.*) so the whole-file replace pattern is never scored.

false_positives.what_happened: Platform-split implementation, not copy-paste. The same OS:: function set (shouldProcessSignal, forwardForeignSignal, sendSignalWithCookie, primeSignalOriginCheck, signalOriginCheckEnabled) is defined in both os_linux.cpp (real logic) and os_macos.cpp (no-op stubs returning accept-all). Identical signatures + similar stub bodies look like a clone but are the mandatory cross-platform overload pair for one interface (os.h).
false_positives.fix_proposal: Suppress cross-file copy-paste between files that are platform variants of the same base name (foo_linux.cpp / foo_macos.cpp / foo_<arch>.cpp) implementing identically-named functions declared in a shared header. Detect via filename-suffix tokenset {linux,macos,windows,bsd,x64,aarch64,arm,i386,...}; if two files differ only by such a suffix, exempt the pair from cross-file scoring.

false_positives.what_happened: Unit-test boilerplate. 16 TEST_F(SignalOriginTest, ...) cases with near-identical 3-5 line bodies (arrange siginfo, call shouldProcessSignal, EXPECT_TRUE/FALSE). The fixture-setup + single-assert shape repeats by design (table of cases), not as drifted production logic.
false_positives.fix_proposal: Apply a lower copypaste weight (or a higher min-token threshold) to files under test/ directories and to blocks dominated by TEST/TEST_F/EXPECT_/ASSERT_ macro tokens; a run of GTest assertions should not count toward chunk-clone density.

cycle.desc: no include cycle: archcheck --graph reports sccs_cyclic=0, largest_scc=1 over 41 nodes / 23 edges
cycle.evidence: archcheck --graph /home/localadm/oss/_aidev_dense/DataDog_java-profiler => sccs_total:41 sccs_cyclic:0 largest_scc:1. The headers use include guards and a one-directional layering (os.h / thread.h / arch.h leaf headers; profiler.h depends down). No A.h<->B.h back-edge found. Note: 90 external + 52 unresolved includes (system + generated jni/jvmti headers) are not in the author graph, so absence of cycle is genuine for resolved author headers.
